programmes.show_description: 60 years on, Tony's close encounter has become an obsession. Meanwhile, pilot David presents photographic evidence of the UFO he almost crashed into whilst flying near area 51. (S1, ep 1)
Adam, Gwen and Julie recall personal encounters with UFOs, before experts' theories are put in doubt when we learn that each individual has time missing from their encounters. (S1, ep 2)
Aliens From the Deep: A medieval monk battles a massive serpent and the legend of the Loch Ness Monster is born. (S1, ep 5)
Alien Pilots, Carolina Lights and Stone Cold Animals: Did a man in Turkey capture an Alien piloting a UFO? What are the strange lights above Brown Mountain in North Carolina? (S3, ep 14)
UK: Ian and Butzy cross the Irish Sea to the north of England, where destinations include Manchester United's Old Trafford, the home of sporting great George Best. (S1, ep 7)
Ulster: The lads kick off in Belfast, home to the SS Titanic - and a treasure trove of maritime memorabilia. In Cavan, Ian bids for some Croke Park turnstiles. (S1, ep 8)
Caernarfon 2: Caernarfon in North Wales is the destination for David Dickinson and his team today, where a lady gives Michael Hogben a hard time for being stingy. (S10, ep 10)
Rick's Big Check: The shop travels back in time when a map from the late 1800s comes in. Meanwhile, Corey spots a killer Mustang for sale.
Henry Cole, Allen Millyard and Guy Willison are back! This time, they revamp their stall after picking up a vanload of trinkets. (S3, ep 4)
If Ya Got It, Flaut It: Kenny happens upon a familiar vehicle and follows it to Lancaster for an auction, while Rene tries to find new luck after losing in Vegas. (S15, ep 20)
Buyers head to storage unit auctions across California. They are only allowed a brief of glimpse of what's inside before deciding how much they are prepared to bid. (S6, ep 1)
Bloody Battle: Husband and wife team, Brandon and Lori Bernier, travel the United States in search of rare treasures at various storage facility auctions. (S1, ep 1)
Cash Wars: Brandon and Lori go to war over a record producer's storage unit and unearth what could be a priceless piece of rock and roll history. (S1, ep 2)
Booby Traps and Safes: A truly enormous auction explodes when rival bidders bet huge sums of money on forfeited international shipping containers. (S1, ep 1)
Matt Attack: Manic Matt sets out to prove a point in the port of Oakland, gambling early on a container full of liquor before driving up prices. Will his tactics backfire? (S1, ep 2)
Forever Free: Montana's Tom and Nancy Oar prepare for the long, dreaded winter ahead. Jake Herak battles thick fog and mountain terrain on foot in pursuit of elk. (S11, ep 1)
Up Snake Creek: The battle for the Everglades gets personal for Troy and Pickle. Meanwhile, Bill and Tes venture north as Zak revisits his childhood stomping grounds. (S3, ep 7)
A man is ready to liquidate his collection of dinosaur fossils and other quirky items and Scott hunts for maritime treasures while Sheldon eats oysters. (S4, ep 8)
Buyers head to storage unit auctions across California. They are only allowed a brief of glimpse of what's inside before deciding how much they are prepared to bid. (S6, ep 2)
Buyers head to storage unit auctions across California. They are only allowed a brief of glimpse of what's inside before deciding how much they are prepared to bid. (S6, ep 3)
Henry Cole, Allen Millyard and Guy Willison visit top collector Stuart Imber before preparing to sell their latest finds at the Fairford Steam. (S3, ep 5)
Close Encounters of the Fifth Kind: Explore whether contact with extraterrestrial life could have occurred. If so, has this happened in the highest levels of the US government? (S15, ep 7)
When your day job involves the oversight of missile silos, nuclear facilities or a military base, the last complication you want is a close encounter with a UFO... (S1, ep 2)

Caernarfon 3: David Dickinson and his team of antique dealers are in Caernarfon in North Wales, where a 90-year-old lady pushes Karen Dalmenty to her limits. (S10, ep 11)
The Drone Zone: When the gigantic, invisible Bubble raises new questions for the team, possible answers suddenly fall from the sky. (S6, ep 2)
The Ranch Strikes Back: When Katie's sister returns for the first time in 50 years to the ranch where they both experienced terrifying events as children, the ranch reacts. (S3, ep 2)
Belief and Superstition: Science reveals new insights into belief and superstition in the land of the Pharaohs, from finding the long-lost 'Land of Punt' to Tutankhamun. (S1, ep 1)
Egyptian Genius: Journey back in time to understand the brainpower behind Ancient Egypt's revolutionary breakthroughs in every field, from medicine to engineering. (S1, ep 2)
There is no safe place. Every day, all around the world, in the most ordinary places, regular people are spotting UFOs. (S1, ep 1)
Close Encounters of the Fifth Kind: Explore whether contact with extraterrestrial life could have occurred. If so, has this happened in the highest levels of the US government? (S15, ep 7)
Forever Free: Montana's Tom and Nancy Oar prepare for the long, dreaded winter ahead. Jake Herak battles thick fog and mountain terrain on foot in pursuit of elk. (S11, ep 1)
Up Snake Creek: The battle for the Everglades gets personal for Troy and Pickle. Meanwhile, Bill and Tes venture north as Zak revisits his childhood stomping grounds. (S3, ep 7)
Strike Out: Sibling rivalry is at an all-time high between Seth and Ashley, and when she scores two baseball deals that are clearly outside her comfort zone, Seth cries foul. (S9, ep 12)
New. Join antiques expert and presenter David Dickinson as more collectors try to sell their items to dealers. Will they strike a deal or take a chance at auction instead? (S9, ep 31)
Caernarfon 3: David Dickinson and his team of antique dealers are in Caernarfon in North Wales, where a 90-year-old lady pushes Karen Dalmenty to her limits. (S10, ep 11)
Declaration Of Indepawndence: Rick heads to New York City to check out an original broadside of the Declaration of Independence. (S24, ep 19)

Buyers head to storage unit auctions across California. They are only allowed a brief of glimpse of what's inside before deciding how much they are prepared to bid. (S6, ep 3)
Brawlin' on the Bayou: Brandon and Lori look to cash in on their first big trip to New Orleans, while the Taylor brothers fight to win a locker loaded with hunting equipment. (S1, ep 3)
Rednecks & Riches: Local legend FJ sees big dollar signs in old voodoo artifacts, while Brandon and Lori unearth a rare find with a potentially priceless payout. (S1, ep 4)
Episode 3: New auctioneer Penny has Israeli young guns Uzi and Schlomi seeing red. (S1, ep 3)
Secret Stash: At the port of Los Angeles, the Uzi and Shlomi are out to destroy the competition. (S1, ep 4)
No Turning Back: Veteran trapper Tom Oar and partner Sean McAfee are pushed to the limit as they battle the elements and unforgiving terrain to set a winter trap line. (S11, ep 2)
Calf Eater: Bruce Mitchell joins the fight alongside Zak and Aaron. Meanwhile, Troy, Pickle, Bill and Tes team up to help a rancher with a serious problem. (S3, ep 8)
In Nova Scotia, Scott pays top dollar for a mechanical rooster and two treasures are found inside a decrepit storage barn. (S4, ep 9)
Buyers head to storage unit auctions across California. They are only allowed a brief of glimpse of what's inside before deciding how much they are prepared to bid. (S6, ep 4)
Return Of The King Of Montebello: Jarrod and Brandi split up for auctions in Montebello. Ivy hires someone to run his shop. Will his new employee be a help or a hindrance? (S6, ep 5)
The vintage restorers return for season four, challenging themselves to make money out of other people's automotive junk and classic collectables. (S4, ep 7)
The Mysteries of Alaska: The vast Alaskan wilderness is known for disappearances, stories of strange creatures and UFO sightings. Could this be due to an otherworldly presence? (S15, ep 8)
These are first-hand experiences of the most terrifying kind: stories shot through with mystery, fear and endless questions. (S1, ep 3)

Caernarfon 4: The dealers are in Caernarfon in North Wales, where someone gives the experts a run for their money when she knows the exact evaluation for her lighter figurine. (S10, ep 12)
The vintage restorers return for season four, challenging themselves to make money out of other people's automotive junk and classic collectables. (S4, ep 7)
Blind Spot: Mike and Avery try to get an auctioneer to help them downsize their collections but Avery scares him off. (S5, ep 6)
Do You Know Who I Am?: An impatient SUV driver in Oregon attacks a cyclist at a red light and gets a serious comeuppance. A driver chases down a fleeing driver who hit her. (S1, ep 5)
All Keyed up: A distracted police officer in Missouri strikes a bicyclist with his vehicle and woman is caught red-handed keying another person's car in Louisiana. (S1, ep 6)
